text: Hip strengthening exercises showed consistent improvements across all outcome measures for ITBS rehabilitation in female runners, never scoring less than stretching or conventional exercise groups.

Hip strengthening exercises produced statistically significant within-group improvements in Y-balance test composite score, dynamometer strength (injured and non-injured leg), LEFS, NPRS pain scores, and single-limb mini squat for ITBS patients.
Hip strengthening exercises showed statistically significant differences compared to ITB stretching alone for ITBS rehabilitation outcomes.
ITB stretching alone produced significant improvements in Y-balance test anterior reach and lower extremity functional scale but fewer overall improvements compared to hip strengthening for ITBS.

No statistically significant between-group differences were found among ITB stretching, conventional exercise, and hip strengthening for ITBS rehabilitation, though the study was likely underpowered with only 24 participants across three groups.

An 8-week rehabilitation program incorporating hip strengthening exercises can improve pain, lower extremity function, dynamic balance, and single-limb squat performance in runners with iliotibial band syndrome.
